title: interface vs type
description: Two ways to describe object shapes.

questions.explanation: Interfaces merge with the same name. Types don't.

questions.prompt: Which can represent primitives or unions?
questions.explanation: type X = 'a' | 'b' — interfaces can't do that.

questions.code:
interface User { name: string }
interface User { age: number }
questions.explanation: Merging can be useful (module augmentation), risky in app code.

options: interface B extends A vs type B = A & { … }
Use type by default; reach for interface when you need declaration merging or clean OO extension
questions.explanation: Community leans on type for flexibility; interface for library shapes.
